archive.title: The Constituency Archive
archive.subtitle: Pre-2008 delimitation maps of India's state legislative assemblies

states.name: Arunachal Pradesh
states.note: Delimitation under the 2002 Act was deferred here in 2008.

states.name: Assam
states.note: Delimitation under the 2002 Act was deferred here in 2008.

states.name: Jammu & Kashmir
states.note: Delimited separately; a 2022 order under the Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act now applies.

states.name: Jharkhand
states.note: Excluded from the 2002-Act exercise by the Delimitation (Amendment) Act, 2008.

states.name: Manipur
states.note: Delimitation under the 2002 Act was deferred here in 2008.

states.name: Nagaland
states.note: Delimitation under the 2002 Act was deferred here in 2008.

states.name: Telangana
states.note: Constituted in 2014; its pre-2008 constituencies fall under undivided Andhra Pradesh.

homepage.title: The assembly maps India retired in 2008
homepage.lede: In 2008, the Delimitation Commission redrew Assembly constituencies across most of India. The electoral map that existed for nearly three decades gradually disappeared from the public record, surviving only in scattered reports and archived documents. Boundaries reconstructs and publishes those historical constituencies as an interactive public archive.
about_body: The Delimitation Commission's 2008 order replaced the Assembly boundaries used across most of India since the 1970s. Those maps remain part of election records, court cases, census publications and political history, but there has never been a single place to view them.

Boundaries is an attempt to assemble that record. Each state is reconstructed from official sources and published as an interactive reference, together with notes on what changed after delimitation.

Created by Deepesh Gulgulia.
